design_philosophy.core_loop: Destroy bases in semi-open stages, collect money, purchase weapon/engine/bomb upgrades in shop, defeat stage boss, progress to next stage. Final stage is boss rush against all previous bosses.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management: balance between spending money on upgrades vs. conserving it; time pressure in shop phases; permanent loss of upgrades upon death creates stakes.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot a sentient, customizable spaceship (Opa-Opa) through fantastical planets, growing stronger through commercial transactions rather than procedural leveling.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Establishes the 'cute 'em up' subgenre by combining shmup mechanics with whimsical character design and non-linear stage exploration. Pioneered the boss rush format. Introduces economic upgrade system to traditionally combat-focused shooter genre, making progression feel mercantile and accumulative rather than skill-gated.

difficulty.lives_system: Player can purchase extra lives with collected money; lives are lost individually and all upgrades reset upon death, creating meaningful consequence.
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-stage checkpoints; stages loop horizontally but progression is stage-based. Death returns player to stage start without upgrades.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player controls spending priority and upgrade selection. Can choose to tank early stages with weak weapons to save money for later stages, or invest heavily early. Time limits in shop phases force decision commitment.

progression.upgrade_system: Tiered purchase system: weapons (with time limits or limited ammo), bombs (limited quantity), engine upgrades (permanent speed increases). Prices rise with each purchase. Player can reassign equipped items by revisiting shop. Weapons and bombs lost on death; engines permanently retained.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Bases must be destroyed to spawn stage boss. All 8 bases defeated → boss appears. Defeating boss unlocks next stage. Final stage is locked boss rush that requires clearing all previous stages.
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon/bomb/engine combinations create different playstyle permutations
Boss rush stage offers skill-based challenge for high-score attempts
Semi-open stage layout allows for different routing and base destruction orders
Difficulty/reward tradeoff: different spending strategies yield different run viability

world_building_techniques: Named protagonist (Opa-Opa, 'Sega's first mascot character') establishes personality
Fantasy Zone setting with alien planets implies invasion narrative
Cute aesthetic and character design contrasts with traditional sci-fi shooter tropes

boss_design.philosophy: Each stage culminates in a boss encounter. Boss rush finale (final stage) rematch all previous bosses in succession before a final boss, creating escalating difficulty and player familiarity with patterns.
notable_bosses: Final boss encountered after boss rush sequence
Master System version features two alternate replacement bosses

faction_indicators: Shop mechanic establishes commerce as central gameplay loop—player aligns with mercantile progression philosophy rather than pure combat mastery
danger_signals: Upgrade loss on death creates resource anxiety; players signal risk tolerance through spending patterns
Time-limited weapons create urgency signaling mechanic stress
environmental_storytelling: Shop balloons mark safe trading zones within hostile stages
Base destruction objectives establish invasion/defense narrative context
Semi-open stage design with looping boundaries suggests contained planetary battlefields
